records.ety: [Formerly also juncate, fr. It. giuncata cream cheese, made in a wicker or rush basket, fr. L. juncus a rush. See 2d Junk, and cf. Juncate.]

defs: A cheese cake; a sweetmeat; any delicate food.
quotes: How Faery Mab the junkets eat.
Victuals varied well in taste, And other junkets.
A new jaunt or junket every night.

defs: A trip made at the expense of an organization of which the traveller is an official, ostensibly to obtain information relevant to one's duties; especially, a trip made by a public official at government expense. The term is sometimes used opprobriously, from a belief that such trips are often taken for private pleasure, and are therefore a waste of public money; as, a congressional junket to a tropical country.
To feast; to banquet; to make an entertainment; -- sometimes applied opprobriously to feasting by public officers at the public cost.
quotes: Job's children junketed and feasted together often.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Junketed; p. pr. & vb. n. Junketing.]

defs: To give entertainment to; to feast.
quotes: The good woman took my lodgings over my head, and was in such a hurry to junket her neighbors.
